---
title: "aa_wilcoxon_timepoint_d10_vs_d5 â€” Olive report"
subtitle: "ribostall global_occupancy â€” occupancy stats"
date: 2026-06-04
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    embed-resources: true
    fig-cap-location: bottom
  pdf:
    toc: true
    fig-cap-location: bottom
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{needspace}
tbl-colwidths: [34, 22, 22, 22]
execute:
  echo: false
---

## Composite figure

![Three-panel bar plot, A-site / P-site / E-site left to right, of per-amino-acid log2 fold-change (median day_10 / median day_5) in whole-transcriptome occupancy. Within each panel the 20 amino acids are sorted by log2 fold-change high to low; bars are coloured by amino-acid physicochemical class (acidic, basic, hydrophobic, neutral, polar; see legend). Y-axis is log2 fold-change, range ~= -0.15 to +0.15; bars above zero are day_10-enriched, below zero are day_10-depleted.](../plots/between_timepoint/d10_vs_d5/composite/APE_aa_Day10_vs_Day5_barplot_composite.png){fig-pos="H" width=92%}

Each panel ranks the 20 amino acids by signed log2 fold-change at one ribosome site (A, P, E): a bar above zero means more day_10 occupancy than day_5 at that site, below zero means less. No significance markers are drawn on the bars.

---

## Overview

- **CSV:** `aa_wilcoxon_timepoint_d10_vs_d5.csv`
- **Family (from `_INDEX.md`):** `between_timepoint_wilcoxon`
- **Sister CSV(s):**
  - `codon_wilcoxon_timepoint_d10_vs_d5.csv`
  - `aa_wilcoxon_timepoint_d10_vs_d0.csv`
  - `codon_wilcoxon_timepoint_d10_vs_d0.csv`
  - `aa_wilcoxon_timepoint_d5_vs_d0.csv`
  - `codon_wilcoxon_timepoint_d5_vs_d0.csv`
- **Test:** Mann-Whitney U (two-sample Wilcoxon rank-sum), day_10 vs day_5
- **Design:** n=4 day_10 vs n=4 day_5 (each n=4 arm pools 2 BWM + 2 control replicates within the timepoint); two-sided Mann-Whitney U on per-(site, amino acid) occupancy; BH-FDR per A/P/E site (each site is its own family of 20 amino-acid tests); effect column is `log2_FC` = log2(median_day_10 / median_day_5).

### Family-level caveats

- **mw-floor-blocking** â€” the Mann-Whitney two-sided p-floor for n=4 vs n=4 is exactly 0.02857; with per-site BH families of ~20 (aa) or ~61 (codon), clearing FDR<0.05 is mathematically near-impossible unless ~every test ties at the floor.
- **condition-pooled-confound** â€” n=4 per timepoint is built by pooling 2 BWM + 2 control replicates (`rep_to_timepoint`); if BWM and control diverge in their time response, pooling masks divergent signals. All 6 members share this design.
- **n=4-low-power** â€” n=4 vs n=4 Mann-Whitney has very low power; nulls across all 6 files are weakly informative.
- **p-floor-aware-headline** â€” Headlines must explicitly state min raw p ~= 0.0286 and explain why "no FDR hits" is uninformative, not a biological negative.

---

## Headline

0 of 60 day_10-vs-day_5 amino-acid tests are significant at FDR<0.05; minimum adjusted p is 0.5714. A single cell reaches raw p<0.05 â€” P-site I (`log2_FC` -0.1469, depleted in day_10) â€” and it sits exactly at the n=4-vs-n=4 two-sided Mann-Whitney floor (0.02857), the smallest p the test can return at this N. The absence of FDR hits is therefore a structural floor-blocking outcome, not a biological negative. This is a coordinated floor-limited null. As nominal-only exploratory leads: P-site I is the only nominally-significant cell; the largest-magnitude enriched cell, P-site G (+0.1524), sits just above the floor (raw p 0.0571); site E is the flattest site (every E-site cell has p_adj 1.0).

## Top hits

Effect column is `log2_FC` = log2(median day_10 / median day_5): positive means day_10-enriched, negative means day_10-depleted. `p_adj` is the Mann-Whitney p after BH correction within each site's family of 20 amino acids (the raw Mann-Whitney p per cell is summarised under Numbers at a glance; min raw p = 0.0286 = the n=4 floor). Each site is split into one sub-table per direction. No row reaches FDR<0.05, so every row below is a non-significant lead; within each sub-table the rows are the up-to-5-per-direction amino acids selected by smallest raw p, displayed by `|log2_FC|` descending. The single row flagged `nominal-only` (P-site I) has raw p < 0.05, and it sits exactly at the n=4-vs-n=4 two-sided Mann-Whitney floor (0.02857), so its raw significance is the maximum the test can produce at this N, not evidence of a strong effect.

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### A site â€” Enriched (day_10 > day_5)

| amino acid     | effect (`log2_FC`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| ---            | ---                | ---                  | ---  |
| G (glycine)    | +0.1299            | 0.8571               |      |
| W (tryptophan) | +0.1063            | 0.9796               |      |
| R (arginine)   | +0.0463            | 0.8571               |      |
| C (cysteine)   | +0.0361            | 0.8571               |      |
| S (serine)     | +0.0152            | 0.9796               |      |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### A site â€” Depleted (day_10 < day_5)

| amino acid     | effect (`log2_FC`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| ---            | ---                | ---                  | ---  |
| K (lysine)     | -0.1202            | 0.8571               |      |
| Q (glutamine)  | -0.1162            | 0.8571               |      |
| N (asparagine) | -0.0671            | 0.8571               |      |
| T (threonine)  | -0.0373            | 0.8571               |      |
| M (methionine) | -0.0186            | 0.8571               |      |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### P site â€” Enriched (day_10 > day_5)

| amino acid     | effect (`log2_FC`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| ---            | ---                | ---                  | ---  |
| G (glycine)    | +0.1524            | 0.5714               |      |
| D (aspartate)  | +0.1213            | 0.9143               |      |
| E (glutamate)  | +0.0840            | 0.9143               |      |
| P (proline)    | +0.0418            | 0.9143               |      |
| C (cysteine)   | +0.0349            | 0.9143               |      |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### P site â€” Depleted (day_10 < day_5)

| amino acid     | effect (`log2_FC`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag         |
| ---            | ---                | ---                  | ---          |
| I (isoleucine) | -0.1469            | 0.5714               | nominal-only |
| L (leucine)    | -0.0740            | 0.9143               |              |
| M (methionine) | -0.0649            | 0.9143               |              |
| S (serine)     | -0.0374            | 0.9143               |              |
| V (valine)     | -0.0176            | 0.9143               |              |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### E site â€” Enriched (day_10 > day_5)

| amino acid     | effect (`log2_FC`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| ---            | ---                | ---                  | ---  |
| P (proline)    | +0.0894            | 1.0000               |      |
| S (serine)     | +0.0671            | 1.0000               |      |
| T (threonine)  | +0.0325            | 1.0000               |      |
| H (histidine)  | +0.0236            | 1.0000               |      |
| A (alanine)    | +0.0185            | 1.0000               |      |

```{=latex}
\needspace{14\baselineskip}
```

### E site â€” Depleted (day_10 < day_5)

| amino acid     | effect (`log2_FC`) | adjusted p (`p_adj`) | flag |
| ---            | ---                | ---                  | ---  |
| R (arginine)   | -0.0590            | 1.0000               |      |
| E (glutamate)  | -0.0388            | 1.0000               |      |
| C (cysteine)   | -0.0320            | 1.0000               |      |
| V (valine)     | -0.0267            | 1.0000               |      |
| N (asparagine) | -0.0034            | 1.0000               |      |

### Flag glossary

- `nominal-only` â€” raw p < 0.05 but `p_adj` >= 0.05. The row would have been flagged significant if tested in isolation, but did not survive BH-FDR correction across its grouping family. The single `nominal-only` cell in this file (P-site I) sits exactly at the n=4-vs-n=4 two-sided Mann-Whitney floor (raw p 0.02857), so its raw significance is the test's discrete minimum, not a measure of effect size.

## Numbers at a glance

- `n_tests`: 60 (20 per site, A/P/E)
- `n_significant` (adjusted-p < 0.05): 0
- `n_significant` (adjusted-p < 0.10): 0
- `min adjusted-p`: 0.5714 (site P)
- `min raw p`: 0.0286 (= the n=4 floor; 1 cell, P-site I)
- `p_floor`: 0.02857 (theoretical two-sided Mann-Whitney floor for n=4 vs n=4 = 2/C(8,4)). One cell reaches it; site E does not reach it at all (min raw p there is higher, all p_adj 1.0).
- Per site: site A min `p_adj` 0.8571; site P min `p_adj` 0.5714; site E min `p_adj` 1.0000.

## Biological interpretation

**No day_10-vs-day_5 difference is detectable at amino-acid resolution, and the null is structural rather than biological (concrete).** None of the 60 (site, amino acid) tests clears BH within its 20-amino-acid site family; the minimum adjusted p is 0.5714. The reason is the test's own arithmetic: at n=4 vs n=4 the two-sided Mann-Whitney floor is 0.02857, and the single cell that reaches that floor BH-corrects to `p_adj` 0.5714 â€” clearing FDR<0.05 would require roughly a dozen amino acids at one site to tie at the floor simultaneously. So 0 of 60 is the design's ceiling, not evidence that day_10 and day_5 occupancy are equal. This is the flattest amino-acid contrast in the `between_timepoint_wilcoxon` family: only one cell reaches the floor (the other two contrasts reach it at four and five cells), and site E is uniformly at `p_adj` 1.0. Pooling further limits what the test can see: each n=4 arm mixes 2 BWM + 2 control replicates, so a day-axis shift that points in opposite directions in the two conditions cancels inside the same comparison.

**The only sub-0.05 raw-p cell is a single floor tie, an isoleucine depletion at the P site (exploratory).** P-site I (-0.1469) is the lone `nominal-only` cell; it sits exactly at the 0.02857 floor, so its raw significance is the maximum the test can return at this N, not a measure of effect strength. It is a directional lead to carry into a higher-powered test, not a hit. There is no obvious mechanistic anchor for an isolated P-site isoleucine drop between day_5 and day_10, so it is most consistent with the discrete-floor noise the caveats describe; its value is purely as a cell to look for again where N is larger.

**The largest-magnitude enriched cells are glycine at both the A and P sites, moving in the same direction but above the floor (exploratory).** P-site G is the file's largest enriched cell (+0.1524) and the only cell besides P-site I that comes close to the floor (raw p 0.0571, just above the 0.05 nominal threshold); A-site G (+0.1299) is the largest enriched cell at the A site in the same day_10 direction. A coordinated nudge toward A-site and P-site glycine at day_10 relative to day_5 is internally coherent as a pattern, but neither cell clears even the raw-p floor, so this is a directional observation, not a finding. It is the cleanest thing in the file to cross-check: codon resolution would say whether both glycine signals localise to one Gly synonym (a codon-usage shift) or spread across synonyms (an amino-acid-level nudge).

**Site E carries no exploratory signal (concrete).** Every E-site cell sits at `p_adj` 1.0 and the panel has the narrowest dynamic range of the three sites; whatever modest day_10-vs-day_5 modulation the pooled design can register concentrates at the A and P sites, not E. Whether this A/P-over-E pattern is real or an artefact of the floor structure is a question for a higher-powered test on the same time axis, not something this file can settle.

**What to carry forward.** Concrete claim to quote: this file shows no signal at FDR<0.05 for day_10-vs-day_5 amino-acid occupancy with conditions pooled, the absence is structural (n=4 floor-blocking) rather than biological, and it is the flattest amino-acid contrast in the family (one floor cell; site E uniformly `p_adj` 1.0). Exploratory patterns worth checking in sister tests: (1) does the lone floor cell, P-site I depletion, reappear with the same direction in the within-condition Fisher `timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d5`, which holds condition fixed and tests the same time axis at whole-transcriptome N, or in the `aa_wilcoxon_timepoint_d5_vs_d0` contrast that shares the day_5 anchor? (2) does the codon-resolution sister `codon_wilcoxon_timepoint_d10_vs_d5.csv` localise the A-site and P-site glycine enrichment to one Gly synonym? Noise to ignore: every E-site cell (all `p_adj` 1.0) and any A/P-site cell with `p_adj` >= 0.857.

## Methods

Columns present: `site`, `amino_acid`, `median_day_10`, `median_day_5`, `log2_FC`, `U_stat`, `p_value`, `p_adj`. The test is a two-sample Wilcoxon rank-sum (Mann-Whitney U) comparing day_10 vs day_5 occupancy per (site, amino acid) at n=4 vs n=4. The `U_stat` column and the paired median columns are consistent with that test. The correction column is `p_adj` (BH within each site's 20-feature family, per `merge_global_occupancy_analysis.py`). The test compares the two timepoint distributions per cell; it does not separate the BWM and control replicates that are pooled into each n=4 arm, and at n=4 vs n=4 a single FDR-significant cell would require ~12+ features tied at the floor simultaneously.

## What the caveats mean for reading this file

- **mw-floor-blocking.** The n=4-vs-n=4 two-sided Mann-Whitney floor is 0.02857; even a cell at the floor BH-corrects to `p_adj` 0.5714 in a 20-amino-acid per-site family, so "0 of 60 at FDR<0.05" is the design's structural ceiling â€” do not read it as evidence that day_10 ~= day_5.
- **condition-pooled-confound.** Each n=4 arm pools 2 BWM + 2 control replicates; a BWM-specific and a control-specific time response that point in opposite directions cancel inside the same comparison, so cross-check the within-condition Fisher (`timepoint_fisher_within_condition_d10_vs_d5`) before reading any null cell as "no day-axis change."
- **n=4-low-power.** With n=4 vs n=4, a real effect can be missed; treat this null as weakly informative, not strong evidence of equivalence.
- **p-floor-aware-headline.** The headline reports min raw p ~= 0.0286 (the achievable floor) and explains why the FDR null is structural; read the 0 of 60 as "by design," not as a biological negative.

---

## Plots â€” individual

![A-site day_10-vs-day_5 amino-acid bar plot. X-axis is the 20 amino acids ordered by log2 fold-change high to low (G, W, R, C, F, S, I, V, E, H, A, M, D, T, P, L, Y, N, Q, K); y-axis is log2 fold-change, range ~= -0.13 to +0.13; bars coloured by amino-acid class (see legend). Largest enrichment at G (~+0.13); largest depletions at K (~-0.12) and Q (~-0.12).](../plots/between_timepoint/d10_vs_d5/individual/site_A_aa_Day10_vs_Day5_barplot.png)

![P-site day_10-vs-day_5 amino-acid bar plot. X-axis is the 20 amino acids ordered by log2 fold-change high to low (G, D, E, P, A, C, R, T, W, V, F, S, H, Y, N, K, Q, M, L, I); y-axis is log2 fold-change, range ~= -0.15 to +0.15; bars coloured by amino-acid class. Largest enrichment at G (~+0.15, the file's only near-floor enriched cell); largest depletion at I (~-0.15, the lone floor cell).](../plots/between_timepoint/d10_vs_d5/individual/site_P_aa_Day10_vs_Day5_barplot.png)

![E-site day_10-vs-day_5 amino-acid bar plot. X-axis is the 20 amino acids ordered by log2 fold-change high to low (P, F, S, L, Y, T, M, H, A, K, G, D, Q, I, N, W, V, C, E, R); y-axis is log2 fold-change, range ~= -0.06 to +0.09; bars coloured by amino-acid class. Largest enrichment at P (~+0.09); largest depletion at R (~-0.06). Site is essentially flat â€” narrowest dynamic range of the three panels.](../plots/between_timepoint/d10_vs_d5/individual/site_E_aa_Day10_vs_Day5_barplot.png)
